desk: WitnessBC Observe — AI policy · governance · startups

items.title: Desk note: what Observe publishes on AI policy
items.excerpt: Scope for the WitnessBC Observe desk — regulator moves, governance patterns, agentic startup signals. Calm tone, documented sourcing, visible corrections.

items.title: Policy brief: why agentic AI projects get canceled
items.excerpt: Analyst forecasts and field signals converge — governance gaps at dispatch, not model quality alone, drive cancellation risk for agent fleets.

items.title: Governance brief: when “that’s policy” blocks accountability
items.excerpt: Opaque AI use policies fail teams twice — no criteria at dispatch and no audit trail after. A governance-first standard: cite, criteria, review path.

items.title: Startup signal: dispatch-time governance as product wedge
items.excerpt: Agentic startups pitching runtime policy gates and signed receipts — pattern-level view of what buyers ask in diligence calls.

items.title: Policy brief: data minimization when agents spread context
items.excerpt: Agent memory and tool chains move PII faster than consent frameworks assume. Minimum standard for safer agent publishing and intake.

items.title: Explainer: an evidence timeline for AI incident reporting
items.excerpt: Date, one sentence, proof type — a calm template for documenting agent incidents without over-sharing identifiers.

items.title: Governance brief: non-response as an unofficial AI policy decision
items.excerpt: When escalation paths go quiet, agents default to allow. Pattern-level look at silence in vendor and internal governance loops.

items.title: Micro: one line that changes AI governance outcomes
items.excerpt: “Can you put that AI use requirement in writing with enforcement criteria?” — a single sentence that converts ambiguity into accountability.
